name: Naan for Breadmaker
recipeInstructions:
1. Make sure your oven is well preheated.
2. Use the dough cycle on your breadmaker.
3. Remove the dough from the machine and divide it into 12 parts.
4. Preheat the oven to 500°F (260°C).
5. Roll each part into a small circle, about 8-inch diameter.
6. Place the rolls on a greased pizza tray or other baking pan.
7. Brush the top of each roll with melted butter.
8. Press finely chopped onions into the dough.
9. Bake for 10-15 minutes, until puffy and brown.
Top Notes:
Sylvia's notes: For best results, use an oven temperature of about 475°F (245°C) if your oven runs hot; rolling the rolls more like 1/8-inch thick works well. Consider adding a spoonful of yogurt-cucumber salad for extra flavor.
Donna German's note: Traditional nan includes onions, but you can also try substituting lemon or orange peel, anise or fennel seeds by sprinkling them on top of the melted butter before baking.
